personal.name: Bhargava Shastry
personal.title: Security Engineer & Researcher
personal.description: Security engineer at the Ethereum Foundation and indie security researcher specializing in smart contract security, fuzzing, and blockchain technology.

experience.title: Security Engineer
experience.description: Conducting security research and development for Ethereum protocol, focusing on smart contract security, consensus mechanisms, and protocol-level vulnerabilities.

experience.title: Independent Security Researcher
experience.description: Conducting independent security research, developing fuzzing tools, and contributing to open-source security projects.

experience.title: Software Engineer
experience.description: Wrote software and tests for Ittiam's Voice over IP phone software.

education.focus: Static analysis and fuzzing techniques for open source bug detection
Specialized in security of microcontrollers and embedded systems
Undergraduate thesis on digital signal processing and embedded systems

themes.title: Ethereum Protocol Security
themes.description: Differential fuzzing and testing tools for validating EIP implementations and consensus-critical code across Ethereum execution layer clients.
highlights: Built differential fuzzers for EIP-7702 (account abstraction) across geth, Nethermind, and Besu
Developed PrecompileFuzzer for testing EVM precompile implementations targeting the Prague hard fork
Created EthFuzzNet, an Ethereum network resilience testing framework
33 commits to goevmlab for EVM trace analysis and test generation

themes.title: Compiler Security
themes.description: Core contributor to the Solidity compiler's testing infrastructure, with 300+ commits focused on fuzzing and correctness testing.
highlights: 303 commits to the Solidity compiler, primarily in fuzzing and testing
Built ABI encoder v2 differential fuzzer
Discovered and reported numerous compiler correctness bugs through structure-aware fuzzing

themes.title: P2P & Networking Security
themes.description: Security testing of peer-to-peer networking stacks used in Ethereum consensus and execution clients.
highlights: Fuzzed libp2p (Rust implementation) for protocol-level vulnerabilities
Built mplex-dos stress testing tool for libp2p multiplexing
Contributed yamux stream multiplexer security patches
Security research on Prysm (Ethereum consensus client)

themes.title: Fuzzing Infrastructure
themes.description: Tools and frameworks for automated vulnerability discovery, contributed to Google's OSS-Fuzz and built standalone fuzzing frameworks.
highlights: Built orthrus, a fuzzing framework for managing parallel fuzz campaigns (216 commits)
69 commits to Google's OSS-Fuzz continuous fuzzing platform
Developed custom protocol-buffer based mutation strategies for structure-aware fuzzing

themes.title: Application Security
themes.description: Fuzzing open-source networking and language runtime software to find and fix memory safety and logic bugs.
highlights: Fuzzed Open vSwitch (16 commits) — found packet parsing vulnerabilities
Contributed to OVN (16 commits) — virtual network security testing
Built mruby proto fuzzer using structure-aware fuzzing techniques
Discovered and reported Boost Filesystem crash bugs

themes.title: ERC-4337 / Account Abstraction
themes.description: Testing and compliance infrastructure for Ethereum's account abstraction ecosystem.
highlights: Built bundler test executor for ERC-4337 compliance testing
Contributed to Holesky funding vault smart contract infrastructure

talks.title: Can A Fuzzer Match A Human: Solidity Case Study
